rulings.Case Title: LARRY TANKS VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order For Order Appointing Successor In Interest As Special Administrator For Purposes Of F.E.L.A. Claims
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 22, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. Plaintiffs' motion for order appointing successor in interest Linda Abraham-Tanks as special administrator for purposes of F.E.L.A. claims is denied. The denial is without prejudice to initiating proceedings in probate court to appoint a personal representative in compliance with 45 U.S.C. section 59. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m., Hon. Lynn O'Malley-Taylor presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY TANKS VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Abate Entire Action Pending Joinder Of Personal Representative Of Decedent Estate
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 22, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Union Pacific Railroad Company's motion to abate entire action pending joinder of personal representative of decedent's estate is granted. An action under FELA may be maintained only by decedent's personal representative. (45 U.S.C. § 59.) It is undisputed that no personal representative has been appointed, therefore no FELA claim may be pursued. This action is stayed pending joinder of a personal representative in compliance with 45 U.S.C. section 59. If and when such personal representative is appointed, plaintiffs shall file a motion to revive the action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m., Hon. Lynn O'Malley-Taylor presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
